prompt: My AGENTS.md files are a mess — there's a 200-line root file with everything jammed in, plus two subdirectory AGENTS files that duplicate half the root content. Please reorganize them. I don't think there are specific session learnings to capture, just clean up what's there.

assertions.text: Root AGENTS.md is significantly shorter than the original (~200 lines) — ideally under 120 lines
Specialized content is moved into topic-specific files (e.g., testing, frontend, backend, security, or similar groupings)
Duplicate content between root and subdirectory AGENTS files is eliminated or significantly reduced
No substantial guidance is lost — all important rules from the original files appear somewhere in the reorganized structure
Response identifies deletion candidates — redundant, non-actionable, or boilerplate content that could be removed
